Assembly: The assembly ilCatPerm1.hap1.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (~https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4), and alignment to GCA_963556745.1.

Assembly: The assembly ilCatFura1.hap2.1 is based on 71x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 8 (0 - 0.5 Mbp) and chromosome 27 (0 - 1.1 Mbp) are unknown.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Crustacea; Multicrustacea; Malacostraca; Eumalacostraca; Eucarida; Decapoda; Pleocyemata; Brachyura; Eubrachyura; Xanthoidea; Eriphiidae; Eriphia
Assembly: Genome assembly of Eriphia verrucosa. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Eriphia verrucosa (qmEriVerr1) is based on 44X ONT data and 76X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Nextdenovo, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 11 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial or viral), totaling 1 Mb (with the largest being 202 kb). Additionally, 1120 regions totaling 136 Mb (with the largest being 909 kb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, no regions were tagged as allelic duplications or contaminants. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilAgrStra1.1 is based on 47x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned by synteny to GCA_928269145.1.

Assembly: The assembly ilAgrGeni1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Some colinearity observed between Z and W chromosomes from hi-c image.

Assembly: The assembly ilAgrTris1.1 is based on 29x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Heterozygous inversion observed on chromosome 1 19.75-29.97mb. Large size differential between haplotypes on several chromosomes observed, with additional sequence not aligning to comparators. 3.3mb of chromosome 13 longer haplotype left in alternate assembly due to difficulty reconciling longer haplotype with the Hi-C map.
